Warning Signs

Signs Your Garage Door Cable Is Broken

A broken cable is a safety emergency. Never try to operate a door with a broken cable — call us immediately for same-day repair.

Door Hangs Crooked or Tilted

If one side of the door sags lower than the other, a cable has snapped on that side. Do not operate the door — it can fall suddenly and cause serious injury.

Door Won't Open at All

Both cables broken means the opener has nothing to pull against — the door becomes too heavy to lift and stays shut or partially open.

Visible Loose or Dangling Cable

You can see a cable hanging loose from the drum or lying on the ground. This is a clear sign of a snapped cable that needs immediate replacement.

Opener Strains or Makes Grinding Sound

When a cable is frayed or about to snap, your opener works much harder to lift the door. Unusual motor noise or slow movement can signal a failing cable.

Frayed or Rusty Cable

If you can see individual wires unraveling or heavy rust on the cable, it's near its breaking point. Replacing it now prevents an emergency — and a much more expensive repair.

Cable Off the Drum

Sometimes cables slip off the winding drum without breaking. The door may open unevenly or get stuck. We respool and retension the cable for proper operation.

Our Process

How We Repair Your Garage Door Cables

Cable repair requires precision and safety — our technicians follow a strict process to ensure your door operates safely and evenly after every repair.

1

Secure the Door First

Before touching any cables, we secure the door in place with clamps to prevent it from falling during the repair — safety is always step one.

2

Inspect Both Cables & Springs

We inspect both cables and the springs simultaneously — a broken cable often signals spring wear too. We give you a full assessment before proceeding.

3

Replace Both Cables

We always replace both cables at the same time. Replacing just one leaves an equally worn cable that will snap soon after — causing another service call.

4

Tension & Balance Test

After installation we test cable tension, door balance, and run full open/close cycles to confirm the door operates smoothly and safely.

Why SolidRise

Why Bay Area Homeowners Choose Us for Cable Repair

We Always Replace Both Cables

Replacing only one cable is a shortcut. We replace both every time — because the second cable is equally worn and will snap just as soon.

6-Month Warranty

Every cable replacement comes with our 6-month parts and labor warranty. If it fails, we come back at no cost to you.

Safety First Every Time

We secure the door before starting and test balance and tension before leaving — we don't cut corners on a repair that directly affects safety.

Same-Day Emergency Service

A broken cable can leave your car trapped or your home unsecured. We dispatch same-day — often within 60 minutes across the Bay Area.

Bilingual Service — Español

Nuestro equipo habla español. We explain the repair clearly in your preferred language — no confusion, no surprises.
